cat /etc/init.d/cron:
#!/bin/sh
### BEGIN INIT INFO
# Provides:          cron
# Required-Start:    $local_fs $remote_fs
# Required-Stop:     $local_fs $remote_fs
# Default-Start:     2 3 4 5
# Default-Stop:      0 1 6
# Short-Description: Cron Daemon
# Description:       This file starts and stops the cron daemon.
### END INIT INFO
# Author:  Ian Jackson <ian@davenant.org>
PATH=/sbin:/usr/sbin:/bin:/usr/bin
DAEMON=/usr/sbin/cron
NAME=cron
DESC="cron daemon"
test -x $DAEMON || exit 0
case "$1" in
  start)
    echo -n "Starting $DESC: "
    start-stop-daemon --start --quiet --pidfile /var/run/$NAME.pid --make-pidfile --background --exec $DAEMON
    echo "$NAME."
    ;;
  stop)
    echo -n "Stopping $DESC: "
    start-stop-daemon --stop --quiet --pidfile /var/run/$NAME.pid
    echo "$NAME."
    ;;
  restart|force-reload)
    $0 stop
    $0 start
    ;;
  status)
    status_of_proc -p /var/run/$NAME.pid $DAEMON $NAME && exit 0 || exit $?
    ;;
  *)
    echo "Usage: $NAME {start|stop|restart|force-reload|status}" >&2
    exit 1
    ;;
esac
exit 0
